creator: nemoliu@gmail.com
text:
my new patch is in the attachment.

On 4/4/07, Stefan Walter <stefan@freebsd.org> wrote:
> Hi,
>
> thank you for your patch. There are a couple of issues with it, though:
>
> - $CATEGORIES is re-ordered. Since the port is in the directory 'java',
>   'java' should remain the the first item in $CATEGORIES, though.
> - $CLASSDIR is removed but still used, which results in snmp.jar,
>   snmpOracle.jar and tablelayout.jar being installed to /, which is
>   definitely undesirable. The right place to put the JAR files is probably
>   the value of $JAVAJARDIR (see /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.java.mk for details).
> - Please consider using $INSTALL_DATA instead of $CP for installing files,
>   since it explicitly sets file permissions.
> - The port respects NOPORTDOCS, which is a good thing, but you removed the
>   %%PORTDOCS%% tags from pkg-plist, so if someone installs the port with
>   NOPORTDOCS set there will be errors upon deinstalling.
>
> Could you send an updated patch which addresses the above issues?
>
> Thanks and regards,
> Stefan
>
